concerns.text: Task T037 implements a 'fallback Wilcoxon signed-rank test logic (only if bootstrap fails)' but omits the specific trigger condition mandated by FR-006. The spec requires the fallback to be triggered 'only if Shapiro-Wilk p < 0.05' (a specific statistical condition), whereas the task description implies a generic 'bootstrap fails' condition (e.g., convergence error). This semantic gap risks implementing the wrong trigger logic, violating the strict statistical protocol defined in FR-006.

Task T029 creates a 'sensitivity analysis stub' for FR-011. FR-011 mandates generating a 'sensitivity analysis report' that either compares logs to objective data or 'explicitly documents the self-report limitation'. A 'stub' (implying an empty or placeholder file) does not satisfy the requirement to generate the report or document the limitation. The deliverable falls short of the FR's acceptance criteria.

Task T029 is placed in Phase 4 (User Story 2 - Compliance Logging). However, FR-011 (which T029 addresses) is explicitly linked to User Story 3 (Post-Intervention Analysis & Reporting) in the spec. The sensitivity analysis requires the final dataset and reporting phase context. Placing this task in US2 creates a logical drift where the deliverable is attempted before the necessary data (post-intervention results) and context (final report generation) exist, violating the data flow constraints of the spec.
